source.source_section: Hypoparathyroidism generated endocrine workup: diagnostic frame

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific.

differentialBuckets.action: Use local assay and patient context; do not apply numeric anchors without clinical interpretation.

redFlags.label: Seizure, laryngospasm, arrhythmia/QT prolongation, severe symptomatic hypocalcemia.

requiredQuestions.label: Post-neck surgery/radiation, paresthesias, cramps, tetany, seizures.

initialTests.label: Calcium plus PTH.

source.source_section: Hypoparathyroidism generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from endocrine workup automation; requires clinician sign-off before validated-intent activation.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. Hypoparathyroidism: low calcium with low or inappropriately normal PTH, often high phosphorus.

initialTests.label: Phosphorus, magnesium, creatinine/eGFR, 25(OH)D.

dispositionRules.action: Symptomatic or severe hypocalcemia changes to urgent calcium replacement and ECG monitoring.

reference_values: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab.
Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific.
DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5.
25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults.
Hypoparathyroidism: low calcium with low or inappropriately normal PTH, often high phosphorus.
